dsl_rule.translation_notes: Mapped 20-day new extremes to Donchian Channels (20-period standard). Entry triggers contrarian reversals: LONG when price makes new 20-day low (close < dc_lower) but closes above entry within bars (close > prev_close, indicating failure to hold low). SHORT when price makes new 20-day high (close > dc_upper) but closes below entry (close < prev_close). Exit at breakout extreme by reversing the entry condition. Stop loss set conservatively at 0.5% given the tight 2-5 bar detection window. Signal focuses on 'Turtle Soup' failed breakout exploitation in ranging/high volatility regimes.

backtest_rule.reason: Mapped 20-day new extremes to Donchian Channels (20-period standard). Entry triggers contrarian reversals: LONG when price makes new 20-day low (close < dc_lower) but closes above entry within bars (close > prev_close, indicating failure to hold low). SHORT when price makes new 20-day high (close > dc_upper) but closes below entry (close < prev_close). Exit at breakout extreme by reversing the entry condition. Stop loss set conservatively at 0.5% given the tight 2-5 bar detection window. Signal focuses on 'Turtle Soup' failed breakout exploitation in ranging/high volatility regimes.

source_rule_text: A trader should enter breakout positions in the direction of new price extremes, but should be aware that obvious breakout setups become crowded and are subject to fakeouts; therefore, improve entry odds by either trading the breakout itself or contrarian entries that exploit the predictable failure of obvious breakouts.
